refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect. The refined FR explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated), incorporates the safety and policy constraints as boundary conditions, and defines the evaluation context (representative operations, agreed period). This makes the requirement testable and traceable to stakeholder needs (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 40 operating hours). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) via onboard instrumentation and/or independent survey. Verify compliance with worksite safety plan via incident log and safety audit. Confirm operational policy adherence via task logs and supervisor review.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks with cycle time and material handling efficiency sufficient to achieve ≥ productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote task execution with latency, control fidelity, and situational awareness sufficient to achieve ≥ productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without degrading productivity below target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time productivity telemetry to the Site Manager and Operator for task monitoring and optimization.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-performance hydraulic drivetrain and bucket control with optimized cycle time logic; couple with tele-operated control path and safety interlock system; measure and report productivity metrics in real-time.

DP-B: Decompose productivity into vehicle performance (cycle time, material handling) and teleoperation performance (latency, control fidelity); design each independently with clear interface contracts; integrate via standardized telemetry and tasking protocols.

DP-C: Implement productivity as an emergent property of vehicle capability, operator skill (on-site or remote), and safety constraints; rely on operator training and worksite procedures to achieve target; measure post-hoc via telemetry.

DP-D: Establish a closed-loop productivity optimization system: vehicle executes tasks, telemetry reports cycle metrics, safety services validate compliance, tasking system adjusts task parameters, operator monitors and intervenes as needed.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Decompose productivity into vehicle performance (cycle time, material handling) and teleoperation performance (latency, control fidelity); design each independently with clear interface contracts; integrate via standardized telemetry and tasking protocols.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time depends on operator input timing; teleoperation latency affects operator's ability to optimize cycle time. Mitigation: define cycle time target independent of operator mode; teleoperation DP must guarantee latency ≤ threshold to preserve vehicle DP effectiveness.

Safety interlock may constrain vehicle performance (e.g., speed limits, bucket angle limits). Mitigation: safety services DP must be designed to enforce policy without unnecessary performance degradation; trade-off analysis required.

Productivity measurement depends on both vehicle and teleoperation; if either fails, target is missed. Mitigation: telemetry DP must capture independent metrics for vehicle and teleoperation to enable root-cause analysis.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B avoids monolithic coupling by establishing clear decomposition boundaries and interface contracts. Vehicle DP is independent of operator mode (on-site or remote); teleoperation DP is independent of specific vehicle model. This supports modularity and reuse. Coupling risks are managed via interface specifications and trade-off analysis.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle cycle time target is achievable with current hydraulic and control technology. Risk: If target is unrealistic, no DP will succeed. Mitigation: validate cycle time target against historical data and engineering analysis before proceeding.

Assumption: Teleoperation latency can be controlled to ≤ threshold (e.g., 200 ms round-trip). Risk: Network conditions may exceed threshold. Mitigation: design teleoperation DP with latency budget and fallback modes (e.g., autonomous task execution, operator override).

Assumption: Safety interlock logic can be implemented without unacceptable performance loss. Risk: Safety constraints may be incompatible with productivity target. Mitigation: conduct safety-productivity trade-off analysis early; involve Safety Manager in DP refinement.

Assumption: Productivity measurement is accurate and repeatable. Risk: Measurement error or variability may mask true performance. Mitigation: define measurement protocol, calibration, and acceptance criteria in advance.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-B is sound but requires explicit sub-DPs for vehicle performance, teleoperation performance, safety enforcement, and telemetry. Recommend proceeding to next decomposition level to detail each sub-DP and establish interface contracts. No revision of DP-B itself is needed at this level.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to deliver productivity. It owns the end-to-end productivity requirement and is responsible for integrating sub-systems, managing interfaces, and ensuring acceptance criteria are met. Ownership: Project Manager and System Engineer.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time, material handled), vehicle status (position, orientation, bucket state), and safety status (interlock state, policy violations) to Site Manager and Operator dashboards.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task commands from Site Manager (e.g., 'excavate zone A') and Operator (on-site or remote). Tasking includes task type, location, material type, and safety constraints.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (e.g., 'interlock triggered', 'policy violation detected', 'operator override required') to Safety Manager and Operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for teleoperation command/feedback loop (Tele-operator to Vehicle) and for telemetry/tasking exchange with Site Manager and Operator.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the entire productivity requirement. It owns the integration logic, interface orchestration, and acceptance criteria. Minimal diffusion: only the measurement/verification logic (telemetry collection and reporting) is partially diffused to Vehicle and Teleoperation sub-modules, but NGHE Solution owns the aggregation and interpretation.

Vehicle owns the core productivity capability (cycle time, material handling efficiency) but does not own the teleoperation path, safety enforcement, or telemetry aggregation. Assigning the entire FR to Vehicle would diffuse teleoperation and safety responsibilities, violating separation of concerns.

Teleoperation owns the remote control path and latency management but does not own vehicle performance, safety enforcement, or on-site operation. Assigning the entire FR to Teleoperation would exclude on-site operation and vehicle performance, making the requirement incomplete.

Safety Services owns policy enforcement and interlock logic but does not own vehicle performance, teleoperation, or productivity measurement. Assigning the entire FR to Safety Services would conflate safety with productivity, violating domain separation.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (System Engineer and Project Manager)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The refined FR (productivity target achievement with safety and policy compliance) is best contained in NGHE Solution because it requires orchestration of vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. No single sub-module owns all aspects. NGHE Solution is the integration point where these sub-systems are coordinated to deliver the end-to-end capability.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' that owns too much integration logic, making it difficult to test and maintain. Mitigation: decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules (e.g., Productivity Manager, Safety Coordinator, Telemetry Aggregator) at the next level, with clear ownership and interfaces.

Risk: Interface contracts between NGHE Solution and sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) may be incomplete or ambiguous, leading to integration failures. Mitigation: define interface specifications (e.g., telemetry schema, tasking protocol, safety interlock signals) explicitly before implementation.

Risk: Productivity measurement may be inaccurate if telemetry from Vehicle and Teleoperation is not synchronized or calibrated. Mitigation: establish telemetry standards and validation procedures; involve Maintenance/Quality in telemetry design.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision of IM assignment is needed. NGHE Solution is the correct container. Recommend proceeding to next decomposition level to detail sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their interfaces with NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR clearly states the productivity target, evaluation context, and boundary conditions (safety, policy). DP-B provides a decomposition strategy (vehicle performance, teleoperation performance, safety enforcement, telemetry) that directly addresses each aspect of the FR. The mapping is traceable: vehicle DP supports cycle time and material handling; teleoperation DP supports remote task execution; safety services DP supports policy compliance; telemetry DP supports measurement and reporting. No ambiguity or missing mappings detected.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry collection logic is partially diffused across Vehicle (cycle time sensors), Teleoperation (latency monitoring), and Safety Services (interlock state). NGHE Solution must aggregate and interpret. Mitigation: define telemetry schema and aggregation logic in NGHE Solution; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services emit standardized telemetry via Telemetry interface.

Tasking interpretation is partially diffused: NGHE Solution receives high-level tasks (e.g., 'excavate zone A'), but Vehicle and Teleoperation must interpret and execute. Mitigation: define tasking protocol with clear semantics; Vehicle and Teleoperation implement task-to-control-command translation.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct container for the FR. It owns the integration logic and interface orchestration. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that implement specific aspects of the DP. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and traceable to the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: The refined FR is verifiable via representative operations trial. Productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h) can be measured via onboard instrumentation (Vehicle telemetry) and independent survey. Safety compliance can be verified via incident log and safety audit. Operational policy adherence can be verified via task logs and supervisor review. All verification methods are feasible and do not require new technology.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The FR is clearly allocated to NGHE Solution, with sub-allocations to Vehicle (cycle time, material handling), Teleoperation (remote control, latency), Safety Services (policy enforcement), and Telemetry (measurement and reporting). Each sub-allocation is traceable to a sub-FR and a sub-DP. Ownership is clear: System Engineer owns NGHE Solution integration; Vehicle team owns vehicle performance; Teleoperation team owns remote control; Safety Manager owns safety enforcement.

recommended_revisions: Refine sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) to the same level of detail as the parent FR. Each sub-FR should have a clear acceptance criterion and verification method.

Define interface specifications (telemetry schema, tasking protocol, safety interlock signals) before proceeding to detailed design. Involve all stakeholders (Site Manager, Operator, Tele-operator, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality) in interface review.
Establish productivity target value (m³/h or ton/h) and evaluation period (e.g., 40 operating hours) in writing. Validate target against historical data and engineering analysis.
Conduct safety-productivity trade-off analysis to ensure safety constraints do not make productivity target unachievable. Involve Safety Manager in analysis.

decision.justification: The refined FR, selected DP, and assigned IM are sound and pass all gates. However, the FR is not yet atomic: it has four sub-FRs (vehicle performance, teleoperation performance, safety enforcement, telemetry) that require further decomposition. Proceeding to the next level will detail each sub-FR, establish interface contracts, and identify implementation modules for each sub-system. This is a normal and necessary step in functional decomposition.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks with cycle time and material handling efficiency sufficient to achieve ≥ productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote task execution with latency, control fidelity, and situational awareness sufficient to achieve ≥ productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan and operational policies without degrading productivity below target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time productivity telemetry to the Site Manager and Operator for task monitoring and optimization.

assumptions: Vehicle cycle time target is achievable with current hydraulic and control technology.
Teleoperation latency can be controlled to ≤ threshold (e.g., 200 ms round-trip).
Safety interlock logic can be implemented without unacceptable performance loss.
Productivity measurement is accurate and repeatable.
Operator skill (on-site or remote) is sufficient to achieve target with proper training.
Worksite conditions (material type, terrain, weather) are representative of typical operations.
Network infrastructure supports teleoperation with required latency and bandwidth.
open_risks: Productivity target may be unrealistic; validation needed against historical data.
Safety-productivity trade-off may be incompatible; trade-off analysis needed.
Teleoperation latency may exceed threshold due to network conditions; fallback modes needed.
Measurement error or variability may mask true performance; measurement protocol needed.
Operator training and worksite procedures may be inadequate; training plan needed.
Integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services may reveal unforeseen coupling; integration testing needed.
evidence_needed: Historical productivity data from similar equipment and operations.
Engineering analysis of vehicle cycle time and material handling efficiency.
Network latency measurements and analysis for teleoperation path.
Safety-productivity trade-off analysis and acceptance criteria.
Measurement protocol and calibration procedures for productivity metrics.
Operator training plan and competency assessment.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies (written).
Interface specifications (telemetry schema, tasking protocol, safety interlock signals).
Integration test plan and acceptance criteria.
